articles.author: Katharine Lang
articles.title: Ultra-processed foods increase 'bad' fatty acids in blood, large study finds
articles.description: Eating a high mount of ultra-processed foods is linked to blood metabolite changes, leading to higher levels of 'bad' fatty acids, according to a large new study.
articles.content:
• There is increasing evidence that ultra-processed foods (UPFs) are harmful to health, but the mechanisms by which they do so are not entirely clear.

articles.title: Consumers get food price relief, but fuel pushes inflation beyond the comfort zone
articles.description: The inflation rate surged to 4.5% in May, but still came in lower than the 4.7% most economists had expected. It is the highest level since July 2024, when inflation hit 4.6%.
